editorNotes: Just like in Great League, Lickilicky is the ultimate neutral pivot and has even more bulk here. Virizion is its only major threat.

Ultra League gives Corviknight more room to flex its bulk. It pairs excellently with other top picks of the format, like Bellibolt or Gastrodon, making it a foundational pick for team building. Similar to Great League, its offenses still leave something to be desired, and its playstyle can be bait-dependent.

Florges echoes Tapu Fini, but brings improved pacing and closing power which make it a top meta contender. Stat effects from either Chilling Water or Trailblaze amplify Florges's neutral play. Florges may have reduced play with the onset of Tinkaton.

Virizion has a long pedigree in Ultra League. Its hard wins against Bellibolt and Gastrodon outshine its potential inflexibility. With Talonflame and Giratina on a significant downturn, it is perhaps at its most meta relevant ever.

Impressive typing and pacing make Empoleon a standout Hydro Cannon user in the format. It's found stronger meta relevancy here in Ultra League, where a wider abundance of Dragon and Fairy types see play. Its lower stat product can easily be shored up by the format's higher bulk and prominent stat-reducing attacks.

Impressive typing and pacing make Empoleon a standout Hydro Cannon user in the format. It's found stronger meta relevancy here in Ultra League, where a wider abundance of Dragon and Fairy types see play. Its lower stat product can easily be shored up by the format's higher bulk and prominent stat-reducing attacks.
Galarian Moltres is still a strong contender in Ultra League, but the Tinkaton's ubiquity poses a major challenge for it.
Similar to Great League, Ultra League Forretress has a sluggish moveset, but even more bulk to work with its debuffing attacks.
Similar to Great League, Ultra League Forretress has a sluggish moveset, but even more bulk to work with its debuffing attacks.

The sims don't always use Chilling Water optimally, so Alolan Ninetales should perform better in some head to head matchups than indicated. Chilling Water is a sidegrade, if not a slight improvement which gives Alolan Ninetales coverage against Fire and broader technical play.
Lapras is a solid choice for Ultra League, although it takes a back seat to other Water types like Jellicent and Feraligatr, which perform better into one another.

Registeel has a storied history in Ultra League. As power creep has made other Pokemon faster and stronger, Registeel is mostly a relic from a bygone age. Its playstyle is difficult to master - it has no Fast Attack pressure and relies solely on its high energy Charged Attacks to secure knockouts. While it could still succeed, its place in the metagame has fallen by the wayside.

Dusknoir combines offensive pressure and great coverage in a strong Ghost-type package. It may not see as much play as more established Ghosts, like Giratina or Drifblim. The Shadow version is typically preferred for the additional pressure.

The sims don't always use Chilling Water optimally, so Alolan Ninetales should perform better in some head to head matchups than indicated. Chilling Water is a sidegrade, if not a slight improvement which gives Alolan Ninetales coverage against Fire and broader technical play.

Dusknoir combines offensive pressure and great coverage in a strong Ghost-type package. It may not see as much play as more established Ghosts, like Giratina or Drifblim. The Shadow version is typically preferred for the additional pressure.
Dondozo adds to the roster of bulky Water types. It hasn't seen widespread play yet, falling behind faster and more flexible picks.

Once the mascot of the format, recent changes have seen Giratina's play decline. It is weak to both of its sub-typings and no longer stands out as the best Ghost or the best Dragon. While its place in the metagame is shaky, it's still plenty competitive. The regular version has the longer tenure, and the Shadow version may be held back by its moderate pacing.
Lapras is a solid choice for Ultra League, although it takes a back seat to other Water types like Jellicent and Feraligatr, which perform better into one another.
Regidrago is a powerful Dragon options with its hard hitting exclusive move. Its poor coverage and average stats keep it from truly excelling.
Talonflame is a longtime staple of Ultra League. The prominence of Water and Dragon types keeps it from dominating, but it serves a vital role against the format's Steel types.
Walrein is a viable and accessible pick, but hasn't resumed its past domination. Jellicent and Feraligatr are more strongly favored in the Water category.
Walrein is a viable and accessible pick, but hasn't resumed its past domination. Jellicent and Feraligatr are more strongly favored in the Water category.
Bellibolt has reduced pacing with Parabolic Charge, but is still a viable pick. Its usage has largely been overtaken by Ampharos.

As one of the longest running mascots of Ultra League, Cresselia could see some resurgence but has thus far failed to find a foothold in the metagame. Top picks like  Corviknight pose major resistance, while its Psychic typing generally hurts it in a format that favors Dark and Ghost types. Still, its stats alone keep it competitive if overshadowed by power creep over the years.
Talonflame is a longtime staple of Ultra League. The prominence of Water and Dragon types keeps it from dominating, but it serves a vital role against the format's Steel types.

Once the mascot of the format, recent changes have seen Giratina's play decline. It is weak to both of its sub-typings and no longer stands out as the best Ghost or the best Dragon. While its place in the metagame is shaky, it's still plenty competitive. The regular version has the longer tenure, and the Shadow version may be held back by its moderate pacing.

Access to Rock Tomb allows Runerigus to shine in Ultra League, where it takes more advantage of its bulk. As such, it's quickly established itself in the metagame. Without Ground-type offenses, it functions more as a neutral pick than a true Ground type like Gastrodon. Its Water weakness can be a liability.

Steelix's unusual mix of fantastic stats, solid typing, and non-STAB attacks give it an unusual role in the metagame. It can power through matchups in unintuitive ways, but its lack of shield pressure and closing power can be more apparent in Ultra League where it faces bulkier targets.

Steelix's unusual mix of fantastic stats, solid typing, and non-STAB attacks give it an unusual role in the metagame. It can power through matchups in unintuitive ways, but its lack of shield pressure and closing power can be more apparent in Ultra League where it faces bulkier targets.

Cradily is not as omnipresent in Ultra League, but still a formidable pick. The format's bulk improves its play with Rock Tomb. However, it faces some sterner resistance, particularly from Empoleon, Virizion, and Zygarde.

As one of the longest running mascots of Ultra League, Cresselia could see some resurgence but has thus far failed to find a foothold in the metagame. Top picks like  Corviknight pose major resistance, while its Psychic typing generally hurts it in a format that favors Dark and Ghost types. Still, its stats alone keep it competitive if overshadowed by power creep over the years.
Ampharos has overtaken Bellibolt as the Electric type of choice, and has a long history in Ultra League. It combines speed and valuable coverage, although its stat product is a bit lacking.
Ampharos has overtaken Bellibolt as the Electric type of choice, and has a long history in Ultra League. It combines speed and valuable coverage, although its stat product is a bit lacking.

Scizor's potent offenses make it a wallbreaker against the format's bulky picks. The Shadow version is typically preferred to increase its power even further. Fire types like Talonflame are declining, so Scizor has even less to fear.

Grumpig sports solid stats and coveted FIghting + Ghost coverage. However, it shares similar struggles to Cresselia and has thus fair failed to find a place in the metagame. Its Psychic typing puts it at a general disadvantage in a meta that favors Ghost and Dark types. All in all, Grumpig has some noteworthy traits but a lot left to prove.

Grumpig sports solid stats and coveted FIghting + Ghost coverage. However, it shares similar struggles to Cresselia and has thus fair failed to find a place in the metagame. Its Psychic typing puts it at a general disadvantage in a meta that favors Ghost and Dark types. All in all, Grumpig has some noteworthy traits but a lot left to prove.

Doublade has yet to see competitive success in any league, so there are many reasons to be skeptical of its Ultra League prospects. The resources required to build it are better invested in its evolution, Aegislash.
